new_value: All review.md findings implemented and validated

old_value: {"id":"fastapi-restly-w12","title":"Fix V2 relation filtering joins","description":"V2 query filtering on related fields mutates a local select_query in helper functions but drops the join before returning, producing cartesian queries instead of proper relationship joins. Add regression coverage for relation filtering and sorting behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-b9v","title":"Reconcile documentation with current behavior","description":"Documentation currently disagrees with the implementation in several places: V2 sorting parameter name, installation instructions, and technical-details references to removed or nonexistent APIs. Update docs after behavior is clarified by code fixes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-i4m","title":"Fix auto-schema relationship detection","description":"Schema auto-generation inspects Mapped annotations but relationship detection checks RelationshipProperty instances, so relationships are not recognized from model annotations. Repair detection and add tests for generated relationship fields or narrow the documented support explicitly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-b3m","title":"Stabilize query modifier version selection","description":"Views cache index_param_schema at include time while query execution resolves the active query modifier version at request time. This can leave validation and runtime semantics on different versions after set_query_modifier_version() changes. Define and enforce a consistent lifecycle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-c4n","title":"Align V2 query parsing with PEP 604 optionals","description":"V2 query parsing handles typing.Union but not PEP 604 unions like str | None. This breaks optional string detection, nested schema traversal, and field-type extraction. Update the parser helpers and add regression tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T13:14:59Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-dd1","title":"Fix V2 relation joins to use relationship attributes","description":"Query modifier V2 resolves nested relation filters/sorts to related model classes instead of relationship attributes. This breaks when multiple foreign keys point to the same table because SQLAlchemy cannot infer the join path reliably. Update _resolve_sqlalchemy_column_v2/_get_sqlalchemy_column_v2 so they return relationship attributes for joins, and add tests covering creator/updater-style double-FK cases for both filtering and sorting.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-6bf","title":"Align V2 pagination metadata with actual query behavior","description":"V2 currently applies page=1 and page_size=100 by default even when the client sends no pagination params, but include_pagination_metadata responses report page/page_size/limit/offset as null in that case. Decide on one contract: either make V2 pagination opt-in, or always surface the effective defaults in the metadata. Add coverage for both metadata and row counts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-cvk","title":"Stop auto-schema from silently coercing unsupported types to str","description":"create_schema_from_model falls back to str for unknown SQLAlchemy/Python types, which can silently corrupt generated API contracts for JSON, Decimal, UUID, enums, arrays, and other custom column types. Replace the fallback with correct mappings where possible and a loud failure for unsupported types. Add coverage for JSON/dict at minimum.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-sez","title":"Generalize IDSchema relation IDs beyond int","description":"IDSchema hardcodes id: ReadOnly[int], but the framework and docs present IDSchema[Model] as the generic way to submit related IDs. Support UUID/string/non-int primary keys for relation payloads, or explicitly narrow the documented contract if generic support is not intended. Add tests for UUID foreign-key resolution.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-c8r","title":"Make quick-start docs runnable on a fresh database","description":"README and getting_started currently present examples as working CRUD apps but omit table creation or migrations, so the first request fails on a fresh database. Update the quick start to include metadata.create_all or an Alembic step, and call out the minimum required setup explicitly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-03-17T15:46:45Z"}

new_value: {"acceptance_criteria":"README and docs describe the new model API clearly.\\nExamples use the recommended naming consistently.\\nDocumentation explains the rationale for separating base semantics from PK/timestamp mixins and reflects the simplified pre-release API.","description":"Revise README, API reference, getting started material, and existing-project guidance so the framework explains when to use dataclass bases, plain declarative bases, ID mixins, timestamp mixins, and any remaining convenience aliases. The docs should make the composition model explicit instead of implying that IDBase is the primary abstraction, and they should document the cleaned-up pre-release API directly rather than a migration story."}
old_value: {"id":"fastapi-restly-jg8.4","title":"Update documentation and examples to explain model base composition clearly","description":"Revise README, API reference, getting started material, and existing-project guidance so the framework explains when to use dataclass bases, plain declarative bases, ID mixins, timestamp mixins, and any compatibility aliases. The docs should make the composition model explicit instead of implying that IDBase is the primary abstraction.","acceptance_criteria":"README and docs describe the new model API clearly.\\nExamples use the recommended naming consistently.\\nDocumentation explains the rationale for separating base semantics from PK/timestamp mixins.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:16Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:16Z"}

new_value: {"acceptance_criteria":"Clear base-class naming reflects dataclass vs plain semantics.\\nPrimary key and timestamp concerns are modeled separately from base semantics.\\nBase helper methods are moved out of the dataclass base.\\nThe public API is simplified directly rather than preserving legacy naming for compatibility.","description":"Refactor the framework-provided SQLAlchemy model base API so the fundamental distinction is dataclass vs plain declarative semantics, while primary-key and timestamp behavior remain orthogonal mixins or convenience aliases. Because Restly has not been released yet, prefer the clearest public API over compatibility shims or transitional aliases. The current naming makes IDBase carry two concerns at once, and Base also contains helper methods that should not live on the dataclass base itself."}
old_value: {"id":"fastapi-restly-jg8","title":"Clarify model base API and separate dataclass choice from PK mixins","description":"Refactor the framework-provided SQLAlchemy model base API so the fundamental distinction is dataclass vs plain declarative semantics, while primary-key and timestamp behavior remain orthogonal mixins or convenience aliases. The current naming makes IDBase carry two concerns at once, and Base also contains helper methods that should not live on the dataclass base itself.","acceptance_criteria":"Clear base-class naming reflects dataclass vs plain semantics.\\nPrimary key and timestamp concerns are modeled separately from base semantics.\\nBase helper methods are moved out of the dataclass base.\\nBackward compatibility strategy is documented and tested.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:01Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:01Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-jg8.4","title":"Update documentation and examples to explain model base composition clearly","description":"Revise README, API reference, getting started material, and existing-project guidance so the framework explains when to use dataclass bases, plain declarative bases, ID mixins, timestamp mixins, and any remaining convenience aliases. The docs should make the composition model explicit instead of implying that IDBase is the primary abstraction, and they should document the cleaned-up pre-release API directly rather than a migration story.","acceptance_criteria":"README and docs describe the new model API clearly.\\nExamples use the recommended naming consistently.\\nDocumentation explains the rationale for separating base semantics from PK/timestamp mixins and reflects the simplified pre-release API.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:16Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-04-01T16:46:31Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-jg8.2","title":"Move model helper methods out of Base into a dedicated reusable helper layer","description":"Extract helper methods such as get_one_or_create and async_get_one_or_create from the dataclass base class into a separate mixin or utility layer. The dataclass/plain base distinction should control declarative semantics only, not whether helper methods exist.","acceptance_criteria":"Base classes no longer own CRUD helper methods.\\nHelper functionality is available from a dedicated mixin or utility with clear semantics.\\nPlain and dataclass users have a consistent story for accessing helpers, or the helpers are intentionally framed as optional.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:16Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:16Z"}

{"id":"fastapi-restly-jg8.1","title":"Design and implement orthogonal base/mixin naming for SQLAlchemy models","description":"Restructure the model base API so dataclass semantics are represented explicitly at the base layer, separate from integer-id and timestamp mixins. Introduce clearer naming such as a dataclass-oriented base alongside PlainBase, and keep convenience aliases only where they improve ergonomics without hiding the composition model.","acceptance_criteria":"The public model API distinguishes dataclass vs plain declarative bases clearly.\\nID and timestamp behavior are applied via mixins or thin aliases rather than being conflated with the base naming.\\nThe resulting API is coherent in imports and generated docs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rutgerprins@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:16Z","created_by":"Rutger Prins","updated_at":"2026-04-01T16:44:16Z"}
